---
title: "Health"
description: "Life expectancy, health spending, system capacity, access, and population health — Canada and OECD peers"
---

Health is both an outcome people care about directly and one of the largest
areas of public spending. These charts pair headline outcomes and spending with
the system's physical capacity — hospital beds, doctors, and imaging equipment —
for Canada and 17 OECD peers.

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar, peer_comparison_line_by_age, single_line

def show_peer_line(path, value_col, yaxis_title, source, initial_start=None, topical=None,
                   initial_visible=None):
    """Standard 17-peer line chart for a World Bank health series.

    `topical` (list of country codes) opens those non-comparator peers already
    coloured + visible — used where the prose singles a country out (e.g. South
    Korea on suicide); every other grey peer is still one legend click away."""
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        print("Data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
        return
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", value_col, title="", yaxis_title=yaxis_title,
        show_average=True, initial_start=initial_start, topical=topical,
        initial_visible=initial_visible,
        source_note=f"Source: {source} | Data as of: {get_data_date(path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Where Canada stands

A scorecard of where Canada ranks among the 17 peers on each measure (latest available year). Dots are ordered by rank, so **further right is more favourable for Canada** (lower-is-better measures are flipped accordingly). Canada is red, key comparators in colour; hover a dot for its value and rank.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import page_snapshot
page_snapshot("health").show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Life expectancy at birth

Life expectancy at birth — the average number of years a newborn
could expect to live if current mortality rates held. It is the most widely used
summary measure of a population's health.

```{python}
#| echo: false
le_path = "data/health/oecd_life_expectancy.csv"
if os.path.exists(le_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(le_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "life_expectancy",
        title="", yaxis_title="Years", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(le_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Life expectancy data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest life expectancy: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(le_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(le_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "life_expectancy", "Years",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(le_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f} years<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## The recent decline

For most of the post-war era, life expectancy rose almost every year. That changed in 2020: Canada's fell from 82.2 years in 2019 to 81.3 in 2022 — the first sustained decline in decades — pushed down by COVID-19 and the [drug-toxicity crisis](substance-use.qmd). It edged back to 81.7 in 2023. The fall was milder than in the United States, shown here for contrast, where the decline was steeper and recovery slower.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(le_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(le_path)
    fig = go.Figure()
    for code, nm, color, width in [("USA", "United States", "#1f77b4", 2),
                                   ("CAN", "Canada", CANADA_COLOR, 3)]:
        d = df[(df.country_code == code) & (df.year >= 2000)].sort_values("year")
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=d.year, y=d.life_expectancy, mode="lines+markers",
            name=nm, line=dict(color=color, width=width), marker=dict(size=5),
            hovertemplate=nm + ": %{y:.1f} yrs (%{x})<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="x unified",
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.04, x=0),
        yaxis=dict(title="Years", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        margin=dict(t=40, b=120),
        annotations=[dict(text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(le_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.18, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Population health & risk factors

Life expectancy summarises the overall outcome; the measures here look upstream — at the behaviours and conditions that shape it, and at mental health. They come from the World Bank's compilation of World Health Organization, UN and International Diabetes Federation data, so all 17 peers are reported on a common basis. By default each chart shows Canada, the named comparators and the peer average; use the legend to add the other peers.

## Suicide

Deaths by suicide per 100,000 population. Canada sits mid-pack among peers at roughly 9 per 100,000 — far below South Korea, the peer-group high, but above Mediterranean peers such as Italy and Israel.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_suicide.csv", "suicide_rate",
    "Deaths per 100,000", "World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory",
    initial_visible=["USA", "GBR"])
```

## Mental health

The share of Canadians aged 12 and over rating their own mental health **very good
or excellent** has fallen from 72% in 2015 to 55% in 2022 — one of the clearest
trends in the national health survey. Unlike the charts above, this is a
**Canada-only** series (Canadian Community Health Survey): self-rated mental health
isn't collected on a comparable basis across the peer group.

```{python}
#| echo: false
mh_path = "data/health/statcan_mental_health.csv"
if os.path.exists(mh_path):
    mh = pd.read_csv(mh_path, parse_dates=["date"])
    fig = single_line(mh, "date", "pct_mh_very_good", title="",
        yaxis_title="% rating mental health very good or excellent",
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x|%Y}</b>: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 13-10-0096-01 (CCHS, age 12+) | Data as of: {get_data_date(mh_path)}")
    fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix="%")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Mental-health data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Tobacco & alcohol

Two everyday risk factors on one chart — use the **Substance** menu (top right) to switch between them. Canada has one of the *lowest* tobacco-use rates in the peer group and sits near the middle of the pack on alcohol. The two use different units, so the y-axis rescales when you switch.

```{python}
#| echo: false
sm_p, al_p = "data/health/worldbank_smoking.csv", "data/health/worldbank_alcohol.csv"
if os.path.exists(sm_p) and os.path.exists(al_p):
    sm = pd.read_csv(sm_p).rename(columns={"smoking_pct": "value"}); sm["substance"] = "Tobacco use"
    al = pd.read_csv(al_p).rename(columns={"alcohol_lpc": "value"}); al["substance"] = "Alcohol"
    sub = pd.concat([sm, al], ignore_index=True)
    fig = peer_comparison_line_by_age(sub, "year", "value", "substance",
        yaxis_title="% of adults", ages=["Tobacco use", "Alcohol"], default_age="Tobacco use",
        hover_suffix="", hover_decimals=1, menu_label="Substance:",
        initial_visible=["USA", "GBR"],
        option_layouts={"Tobacco use": "% of adults", "Alcohol": "Litres per capita (15+)"},
        source_note=f"Source: World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory | Data as of: {get_data_date(sm_p)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Tobacco/alcohol data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Overweight

Share of adults with a body-mass index of 25 or higher. The measure has climbed across nearly every peer over three decades; Canada is around 60%, below the United States but above most of Europe and well above Japan and South Korea.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_overweight.csv", "overweight_pct",
    "% of adults (BMI ≥ 25)", "World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory",
    topical=["JPN", "KOR"])
```

## Diabetes

Estimated diabetes prevalence among adults aged 20–79. The International Diabetes Federation publishes these at intervals rather than annually, so this is the latest available estimate, shown as a ranked comparison rather than a trend.

```{python}
#| echo: false
dm_path = "data/health/worldbank_diabetes.csv"
if os.path.exists(dm_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(dm_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "diabetes_pct", "% of adults 20–79",
        f"Source: World Bank / IDF Diabetes Atlas | Data as of: {get_data_date(dm_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Maternal mortality

Maternal deaths per 100,000 live births (modelled estimates on a consistent international basis). Most peers cluster at very low ratios — a peer median around 4 — but Canada, at roughly 12, sits near the high end of the group; only the United States is higher.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_maternal_mortality.csv", "maternal_mortality",
    "Per 100,000 live births", "World Bank (WHO/UNICEF/UNFPA/World Bank/UNDESA)",
    initial_visible=["USA", "DEU", "GBR"])
```

## Childhood vaccination

The share of children aged 12–23 months immunized against measles (first dose).
Because measles is so contagious, roughly **95%** coverage — the dashed line — is
the widely used threshold for stopping community spread. Canada's coverage sits
around 90%, below most European peers, and coverage dipped across many countries
around the pandemic. (The matching DTP3 series is in the downloads.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
vac_path = "data/health/worldbank_measles_vaccination.csv"
if os.path.exists(vac_path):
    vac = pd.read_csv(vac_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(vac, "year", "measles_immunization_pct", "",
        "% of children 12–23 months", show_average=True,
        source_note=f"Source: World Bank (WHO/UNICEF immunization estimates) | Data as of: {get_data_date(vac_path)}")
    fig.add_hline(y=95, line_dash="dash", line_color="#555", line_width=1)
    fig.update_yaxes(range=[50, 101])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Vaccination data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(vac_path):
    fig = ranked_bar(vac, "measles_immunization_pct", "% of children 12–23 months",
        f"Source: World Bank (WHO/UNICEF immunization estimates) | Data as of: {get_data_date(vac_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.0f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Health spending

Current expenditure on health as a percentage of GDP — covering both public and
private spending. It measures the share of the economy devoted to health care.

```{python}
#| echo: false
hs_path = "data/health/oecd_health_spending_gdp.csv"
if os.path.exists(hs_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(hs_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "health_pct_gdp",
        title="", yaxis_title="% of GDP", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Expenditure (SHA) | Data as of: {get_data_date(hs_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Health spending data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest health spending: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(hs_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(hs_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "health_pct_gdp", "% of GDP",
        f"Source: OECD Health Expenditure (SHA) | Data as of: {get_data_date(hs_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Health spending per person

The same spending expressed **per person**, in US dollars adjusted for purchasing
power (PPP) so amounts are comparable across countries. This is often more
tangible than the share of GDP — it is roughly what the system spends, on
average, for each resident in a year.

```{python}
#| echo: false
hspc_path = "data/health/oecd_health_spending_pc.csv"
if os.path.exists(hspc_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(hspc_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "health_pc_usd",
        title="", yaxis_title="US$ PPP per person", show_average=True)
    fig.update_yaxes(tickprefix="$", tickformat=",")
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Expenditure (SHA), USD PPP | Data as of: {get_data_date(hspc_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Health spending per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest health spending per person: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(hspc_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(hspc_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "health_pc_usd", "US$ PPP per person",
        f"Source: OECD Health Expenditure (SHA), USD PPP | Data as of: {get_data_date(hspc_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.update_xaxes(tickprefix="$", tickformat=",")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Pharmaceutical spending per person

Spending on retail pharmaceuticals and other medical non-durables (SHA function
HC.5.1), in US dollars at purchasing-power parity per person. Canada is among the
highest spenders in the peer group — behind only the United States in most years.
Definitions differ slightly across countries (Canada's figure is flagged by the
OECD as differing in definition), so read small gaps loosely.

```{python}
#| echo: false
rx_path = "data/health/oecd_pharma_spending_pc.csv"
if os.path.exists(rx_path):
    rx = pd.read_csv(rx_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(rx, "year", "pharma_pc_usd", "",
        "US$ PPP per person", show_average=True,
        source_note=f"Source: OECD Health Expenditure (SHA), retail pharmaceuticals (HC.5.1), USD PPP | Data as of: {get_data_date(rx_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Pharmaceutical-spending data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(rx_path):
    fig = ranked_bar(rx, "pharma_pc_usd", "US$ PPP per person",
        f"Source: OECD Health Expenditure (SHA), retail pharmaceuticals (HC.5.1) | Data as of: {get_data_date(rx_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.update_xaxes(tickprefix="$", tickformat=",")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Universal health coverage

Spending measures the money going in; the WHO's universal-health-coverage service-coverage index (0–100) measures how fully a population actually receives the essential health services it needs. Canada scores around 92 — among the highest in the peer group, reflecting broad access notwithstanding the wait-time pressures shown below.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_uhc_index.csv", "uhc_index",
    "Index (0–100)", "World Bank / WHO Universal Health Coverage")
```

## Out-of-pocket costs

The share of total health spending that households pay directly, out of their own pockets — a common gauge of financial protection, where lower means more care is covered by public or pooled funding. At roughly 15%, Canada sits mid-pack: lower than Switzerland or South Korea, higher than France or the Netherlands.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_health_oop.csv", "oop_pct",
    "% of current health spending", "World Bank / WHO Global Health Expenditure Database")
```

## Health-system capacity

Spending and outcomes describe the system's size and results; capacity describes what it physically has to work with — beds, doctors, and diagnostic equipment. These inputs bear directly on access and wait times, and are a common lens for discussions of the Canadian system.

## Hospital beds

Total hospital beds per 1,000 population (all care functions).

```{python}
#| echo: false
beds_path = "data/health/oecd_hospital_beds.csv"
if os.path.exists(beds_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(beds_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "hospital_beds",
        title="", yaxis_title="Beds per 1,000 population", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(beds_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Hospital beds data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest hospital beds: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(beds_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(beds_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "hospital_beds", "Beds per 1,000 population",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(beds_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Practising physicians

Practising physicians per 1,000 population.

```{python}
#| echo: false
phys_path = "data/health/oecd_physicians.csv"
if os.path.exists(phys_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(phys_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "physicians",
        title="", yaxis_title="Physicians per 1,000 population", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(phys_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Physician data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest practising physicians: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(phys_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(phys_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "physicians", "Physicians per 1,000 population",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(phys_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Practising nurses

Practising nurses per 1,000 population. Nurses are the largest part of the health workforce, so this is a central measure of system capacity alongside physicians and beds.

```{python}
#| echo: false
nurses_path = "data/health/oecd_nurses.csv"
if os.path.exists(nurses_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(nurses_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "nurses",
        title="", yaxis_title="Nurses per 1,000 population", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(nurses_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Nurses data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest practising nurses: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(nurses_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(nurses_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "nurses", "Nurses per 1,000 population",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(nurses_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## MRI units

Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) units per million population — a measure of diagnostic-imaging capacity.

```{python}
#| echo: false
mri_path = "data/health/oecd_mri_units.csv"
if os.path.exists(mri_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(mri_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "mri_units",
        title="", yaxis_title="MRI units per million population", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(mri_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("MRI data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest MRI units: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(mri_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(mri_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "mri_units", "MRI units per million population",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(mri_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Long-term care beds

Beds in long-term care facilities and the long-term-care wards of hospitals, per
1,000 people aged 65 and over — the institutional capacity behind aging-population
pressures (see the [age structure of the population](../population/index.qmd)).
Countries organize elder care very differently — some emphasize home care over
institutions — so this measures capacity in one model of care, not the quality of
elder care overall.

```{python}
#| echo: false
ltc_path = "data/health/oecd_ltc_beds.csv"
if os.path.exists(ltc_path):
    ltc = pd.read_csv(ltc_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(ltc, "year", "ltc_beds_per_1k_65plus", "",
        "Beds per 1,000 aged 65+", show_average=True,
        source_note=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics (Long-Term Care Resources) | Data as of: {get_data_date(ltc_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Long-term-care data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(ltc_path):
    fig = ranked_bar(ltc, "ltc_beds_per_1k_65plus", "Beds per 1,000 aged 65+",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics (Long-Term Care Resources) | Data as of: {get_data_date(ltc_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## A regular health-care provider

The share of Canadians aged 12 and over who report having a **regular health-care
provider** — a family doctor or nurse practitioner they usually see. In 2022 it was
86%, which still leaves roughly **one in seven** without one. A **Canada-only**
series (Canadian Community Health Survey, annual estimates to 2022; the survey was
redesigned afterwards, so later years will appear when published on a comparable
basis).

```{python}
#| echo: false
fd_path = "data/health/statcan_family_doctor.csv"
if os.path.exists(fd_path):
    fd = pd.read_csv(fd_path, parse_dates=["date"])
    fig = single_line(fd, "date", "pct_regular_provider", title="",
        yaxis_title="% with a regular health-care provider",
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x|%Y}</b>: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 13-10-0096-01 (CCHS, age 12+) | Data as of: {get_data_date(fd_path)}")
    fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix="%")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Regular-provider data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Wait times for priority procedures

The share of patients treated within the agreed **pan-Canadian benchmark** time, for five priority procedures (Canada-wide). Higher is better — more patients treated on time. The pandemic sharply cut access to scheduled surgeries (hip, knee, cataract) in 2020, and most have only partly recovered since; emergency hip-fracture repair and radiation therapy held up. Unlike most charts on this page, this is a **Canada-only** series — there is no comparable peer feed — and the Canadian Institute for Health Information (CIHI) publishes it **annually each spring**, so it is refreshed once a year rather than weekly with the rest of the site.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import lines_over_time
wt_path = "data/health/cihi_wait_times.csv"
if os.path.exists(wt_path):
    wt = pd.read_csv(wt_path)
    fig = lines_over_time(wt, "year", "pct_meeting_benchmark", "procedure",
        yaxis_title="% treated within benchmark", yticksuffix="%", hover_decimals=0,
        rangemode="tozero",
        source_note=f"Source: CIHI, Wait Times for Priority Procedures in Canada (April–September) | Data as of: {get_data_date(wt_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Wait-times data not yet available. Run: python -m pipeline.build_wait_times")
```

The capacity measures above — beds, physicians, and imaging units — are the closest internationally comparable proxies; the benchmark series here adds the domestic, access-focused view that the public most directly experiences.

## Download data

- [Life Expectancy at Birth (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_life_expectancy.csv) — OECD Health Statistics
- [Health Spending, % of GDP (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_health_spending_gdp.csv) — OECD Health Expenditure (SHA)
- [Health Spending per Person, US$ PPP (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_health_spending_pc.csv) — OECD Health Expenditure (SHA)
- [Hospital Beds (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_hospital_beds.csv) — OECD Health Statistics
- [Practising Physicians (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_physicians.csv) — OECD Health Statistics
- [Practising Nurses (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_nurses.csv) — OECD Health Statistics
- [MRI Units (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_mri_units.csv) — OECD Health Statistics
- [Wait Times — % Meeting Benchmark (CSV)](../data/health/cihi_wait_times.csv) — CIHI Wait Times for Priority Procedures
- [Suicide Mortality Rate (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_suicide.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory
- [Tobacco Use (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_smoking.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory
- [Alcohol Consumption (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_alcohol.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory
- [Overweight (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_overweight.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory
- [Diabetes Prevalence (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_diabetes.csv) — World Bank / IDF Diabetes Atlas
- [Maternal Mortality (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_maternal_mortality.csv) — World Bank (WHO/UNICEF/UNFPA/World Bank/UNDESA)
- [Universal Health Coverage Index (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_uhc_index.csv) — World Bank / WHO Universal Health Coverage
- [Out-of-Pocket Health Spending (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_health_oop.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Expenditure Database
- [Has a Regular Health-Care Provider (CSV)](../data/health/statcan_family_doctor.csv) — Statistics Canada Table 13-10-0096-01 (CCHS)
- [Perceived Mental Health, Very Good or Excellent (CSV)](../data/health/statcan_mental_health.csv) — Statistics Canada Table 13-10-0096-01 (CCHS)
- [Long-Term Care Beds (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_ltc_beds.csv) — OECD Health Statistics (Long-Term Care Resources)
- [Pharmaceutical Spending per Person (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_pharma_spending_pc.csv) — OECD Health Expenditure (SHA, HC.5.1)
- [Measles Immunization (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_measles_vaccination.csv) — World Bank (WHO/UNICEF estimates)
- [DTP3 Immunization (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_dtp_vaccination.csv) — World Bank (WHO/UNICEF estimates)
